title: Do you think NTE would have fared better without Asuka
display_title: Do you think NTE would have fared better without Asuka
wikitext:
[]: Do you think NTE would have fared better without Asuka?             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Dec 03, 2014 7:11 am What do you think, would cutting Asuka have hurt NTE overall, or would it create breathing-room for the other characters? I know we haven't seen Final yet, where she will have her fair share of scenes. (Although I guess, Anno himself has no idea what's going to happen in the end, as of now.) I know they wouldn't have dared to cut Asuka out, because she's just too much of a cash-cow. But just imagine there was no Asuka, and Mari would have gotten all the additional screen time. No one would be unhappy that Shikinami is not Soryu, or that she behaves so differently or that she is a shallow imposter of the original. NTE would have avoided a big chunk of problems. But in cutting her NTE might have lost a great deal of diversity, energy and depth. Discuss.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Dec 03, 2014 8:04 am No Asuka in NTE would mean no kickass Asuka in Q that show us what an Asuka with a big part of her personal issues resolved is like, and that would suck. So no, NTE without Asuka wouldn't had been better.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Wed Dec 03, 2014 8:35 am Shallow imposter? pfft. that would require shikinami to be actively attempting to mimic soryu and not be her own character, and frankly shikinami's growth is one of my favourite parts of Q so no, in no way would i ever want Asuka cut out of NTE

[]: Re: Do you think NTE would have fared better without Asuka?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Dec 03, 2014 10:03 am robersora wrote: What do you think, would cutting Asuka have hurt NTE overall, or would it create breathing-room for the other characters?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Dec 03, 2014 10:47 am Has Asuka actually done anything yet? Anything that she specifically was needed for and that couldn't have been done just as well by random new pilot #7 or whatever? Because I'm sure not seeing anything.

[]: Re: Do you think NTE would have fared better without Asuka?             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Wed Dec 03, 2014 11:14 am robersora wrote: No one would be unhappy that Shikinami is not Soryu, or that she behaves so differently or that she is a shallow imposter of the original.

[]: Re: Do you think NTE would have fared better without Asuka?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Dec 03, 2014 11:19 am TheFriskyIan wrote: Complaints like these are unjustified.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Dec 03, 2014 12:06 pm Without Asuka? No. Without MARI? Yes. While Asuka has had some development as a character, Mari has yet to have a single meaningful conversation with any of the characters other than a presumed offscreen friendship with Asuka, we know nothing about her history or her past aside from being connected to a government agency. She has no real defining traits aside from being 'perfect', and bemoaning her chest size, she adds nothing to the story and hogs precious screen time that could've been used for developing other characters. Couldn't have Asuka piloted Unit 02 wounded in the battle against Zeruel? Couldn't have they just had Asuka be a lone wolf Eva pilot in 3.0? Couldn't have they used the screen time they used giving Mari fight scenes, on developing Shinji and Asuka's relationship, or explaining how the hell Impacts work so we'd be less in the dark when the time skip came in 3.0? No, and the Rebuilds are all the poorer for it.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Wed Dec 03, 2014 12:47 pm Ray wrote: Without MARI? Yes.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Dec 03, 2014 1:02 pm Keep in mind, I don't Hate Mari. I just wish she wasn't so. . .shoehorned in. 20 more minutes, a talk with Kaji, a conversation with anyone really. And I'd like her a lot more.

[]:             Quote Post by GT-One » Wed Dec 03, 2014 1:28 pm One doesn't simply cut out Asuka. Period. Even before I watched Evangelion the first time (which actually have been the new movies prior to the original series), I already knew about that one girl displaying my favorite color and I probably would have found it a bit strange, if I didn't encounter her character at least in the second new movie. And let's face it, Shinji needs someone to get his butt kicked real good, but who could actually do this, if not Asuka? Misato? She's at least good at pretending to be that strong and competent major at work. Rei? Slapping the boy in his face deserves some bonus points, but won't do enough damage in the end. And Mari? I don't mind her being in the new movies or not, but with Rei and Asuka also around, she's a bit of a fifth (third) wheel on the wagon. Still, she deserves (or rather gets) some attention, thanks to her extroverted personality (while also showing off the most girlish plugsuit (by color) in the franchise until then). But Evangelion without Asuka? Someone seems to like his french fries with mustard rather than ketchup. Also, whether Soryu or Shikinami, she's still Asuka Langley and it's not like they completely changed her character traits. In fact, I have to question her lack of screentime in both 'Ha' and 'Q' as I'm not annoyed (in a good way) as much as with her counterpart in the original series.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Dec 03, 2014 1:31 pm Ok, the we all know the Mari-problematic, we have already been there. That's why I suggested the idea of throwing Asuka of the franchise. I know that would make some people hate Mari even more. I guess we would have gotten a lot of complaints in the vein of "who is that bitch, and why is she replacing Asuka?!" (In my opinion, the biggest problem of Mari is, that they've dropped some hints without really being sure what her role will be; and now they don't know how to put the pieces together... For now Sadamoto's take on Mari seems very plausible, but let's see if that translates to NTE) But enough of Mari. If they decided to throw Asuka off, they wouldn't have to shoehorn Asuka in somehow, (the interviews are a testament of how big of a problem they encountered in figuring out Asuka's role in 2.22). They could have used the time for fleshing out the characters just a little bit more (doesn't need to be Mari). In my opinion, Asuka fits into 3.33 very well; so it would be cool if she would have just appeared in the beginning of Q. (of course, a lot of hate geared towards Shinji wouldn't make sense that way, Rei would have to go through her self-identity crises while invading Wunder alone; and pulling out Shinji from the entry plug at the end would have been Mari's duty; or, even more interesting Rei's). But she could still appear, take up a different role and thus separate her character even more clearly from NGE-Asuka's. As it stands, due to the time constraints, I think there is one pilot too many. I would have found it interesting, if they threw Rei off-board, as well. Now that would have changed up the whole narrative quite a bit!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Dec 03, 2014 1:46 pm robersora wrote: a lot of complaints in the vein of "who is that bitch, and why is she replacing Asuka?!"

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Dec 03, 2014 2:21 pm NemZ wrote: Has Asuka actually done anything yet? Anything that she specifically was needed for and that couldn't have been done just as well by random new pilot #7 or whatever? Because I'm sure not seeing anything.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Dec 03, 2014 4:49 pm Mr. Tines wrote: It would have been more honest than what we ended up with, given the development history of her role as revealed by the 2.0 CRC, even if it would have annoyed the fans who seem to prefer the less prickly replacement. There's even a chance that Mari would be more than just Mari's Maris, if you follow my drift.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Wed Dec 03, 2014 4:59 pm ^ Bag said it better than i could.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Dec 03, 2014 5:42 pm You're damned if you do & you're damned if you don't. Though personally I'm fine with Asuka's emotional arch being an offscreen journey. Clearly the Rebuild narrative is not her story to tell. She's merely along for the ride.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Dec 03, 2014 5:50 pm Bagheera wrote: Can't agree. EoE wouldn't have even worked without Soryu, and the whole show would have died at ep 10 if not for her.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Dec 03, 2014 6:01 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I guess Anno needed something brash to distract from the far more important and far more interesting Rei Ayanami character, true.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Dec 03, 2014 6:16 pm Bagheera wrote: You wouldn't know, since you haven't seen the movies in question.

I know we haven't seen Final yet, where she will have her fair share of scenes. (Although I guess, Anno himself has no idea what's going to happen in the end, as of now.) I know they

Article: Do you think NTE would have fared better without Asuka
 1:31 pm Ok, the we all know the Mari-problematic, we have already been there. That's why I suggested the idea of throwing Asuka of the franchise. I know that would make some people hate Mari even more. I guess we would have gotten a lot of complaints in the vein of "who is that bitch, and why is she replacing Asuka?!" (In my opinion, the biggest problem of Mari is, that they've dropped some hints without really being sure what her role will be; and now they don't know how to put the pieces together... If they decided to throw Asuka off, they wouldn't have to shoehorn Asuka in somehow, (the interviews are a testament of how big of a problem they encountered in figuring out Asuka's role in 2.22). They could have used the time for fleshing out the characters just a little bit more (doesn't need to be Mari). In my opinion, Asuka fits into 3.33 very well; so it would be cool if she would have just appeared in the beginning of Q. (of course, a lot of hate geared towards Shinji wouldn't make sense that way, Rei would have to go through her self-identity crises while invading Wunder alone; and pulling out Shinji from the entry plug at the end would have been Mari's duty; or, even more interesting Rei's). But she could still appear, take up a different role and thus separate her character even more clearly from NGE-Asuka's. As it stands, due to the time constraints, I think there is one pilot too many. I would have found it interesting, if they threw Rei off-board, as well. Now that would have changed up the whole narrative quite a bit!
